title: Better document exists-forall problem support
body:
General
====
I don't see much in the documentation aside from:

> FIXME: Add information about $assert,$assume,$live,$fair,$cover,$equiv,$initstate,$anyconst,$anyseq,$allconst,$allseqcells

Some of these have somewhat decent descriptions at https://symbiyosys.readthedocs.io/en/latest/verilog.html#unconstrained-variables .

Questions to Clarify
====
Looking over commit b13e6bd375dc19fc2d6a3e67cdc6c045da732200, I'm not super clear on what exactly are `ex_state_eq` and `ex_input_eq` and their role in the problem formulation.

I suppose that's partly a consequence of my lack of understanding of the whole process for setting up the problem for solving (the yosys-smtbmc part, I guess).  A general overview of how the emitted SMTLIBv2 differs from normal, how yosys-smtbmc treats it differently, etc. would be very helpful.

For example, I see things like (`backends/smt2/smt2.cc`):
```
ex_state_eq.push_back(stringf("(= %s %s)", get_bv(cell->getPort("\\Y")).c_str(), get_bv(cell->getPort("\\Y"), "other_state").c_str()));
```

I wonder what exactly is the purpose of `other_state`.  Is the equality constraint between application with `state` and application with `other_state`, (or, rather with `s0` and `s0_` in `examples/smtbmc/demo8`), what ensures that it is the *same* constant chosen both in the outer (i.e. outside `forall`) and inner (inside `forall`) scope?

However it really works, this seems to be a funny way of defining an exists-forall problem. Doesn't it make more sense to just `declare-const` the existentially-quantified variable?

Also, it seems that `$anyseq` and `$allseq` support sequential circuits--I mean, that's the raison d'etre. Yet `backends/smt2/smtio.py` seems to imply that unrolling circuits in exists-forall problems is not supported.  Is that true?
